commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] No narrow repair lane remains; maintainers need to coordinate the linked producer PRs and accept the Go 1.26.4 baseline before merge.; Cleared: The diff adds a local command execution path, but it is opt-in, uses no shell, binds execution to the selected crawler binary, checks read-only JSON metadata, and decodes a narrow payload; no concrete security... (sha=1a285a6103c28f1f4fa821f8e1b2e7f59b2a013b)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a ContactExport v0 JSON/proto contract, `clawdex import contacts --from`, crawler metadata execution, source evidence in person markdown, import tests/docs, and a Go directive bump to 1.26.4.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this is a new import capability. The review used source inspection, focused tests, and the PR's aggregate copied-real-data proof for the intended behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No narrow repair lane remains; maintainers need to coordinate the linked producer PRs and accept the Go 1.26.4 baseline before merge.

target.title: Import contact exports from source crawlers
target.body:
## Summary

- add the clawdex-owned `ContactExport` v0 proto artifact and strict JSON decoder
- add `clawdex import contacts --from <source crawler>`
- discover `contact-export` through crawlkit metadata, require `crawlkit.control.v1`, require JSON/read-only, bind execution to the same `--from` binary, and import only display name plus phone values into the existing `SourceContact` path
- record simple per-source evidence in clawdex person markdown so an existing phone match can still say it was seen in `telecrawl` or `wacrawl`
- keep crawler imports from auto-merging by name alone; crawler imports match by stronger identifiers such as accounts, external IDs, email, or normalized phone
- make crawler import dry-runs match real imports, dedupe phones by normalized phone value, skip cross-person conflicting phone values, and return `[]` for no-op JSON imports
- align the proto package with the v0 contract: `openclaw.clawdex.contactexport.v0`
- move clawdex to Go 1.26.4 so CI `govulncheck` uses the fixed standard library

## Related PRs

- Consumer: https://github.com/openclaw/clawdex/pull/2
- WhatsApp producer: https://github.com/steipete/wacrawl/pull/12
- Telegram producer: https://github.com/openclaw/telecrawl/pull/9

These three PRs are one contact-export v0 slice. Source crawlers own source-native contact extraction; clawdex owns canonical people and imports by pulling the crawler metadata `contact-export` command. They should land in lockstep; if the command name, metadata argv, envelope, or field names change in one repo, all three should change together.

## Intent

The discoverable local `contact-export` metadata command is intentional. Clawdex discovering and running that read-only command is intentional. Local opt-in import of stored contact display names and phone numbers into clawdex is the feature boundary, not an accidental privacy expansion.

The v0 producer contract is deliberately small: `crawlkit.control.v1`, command name `contact-export`, JSON envelope `contacts`, fields `display_name` and `phone_numbers`, and read-only metadata argv. This PR intentionally does not add graph/candidate/source-id/JID/username/ranking fields or a generic crawler-to-crawler protocol.

Clawdex records local-only source evidence after import. That is not a producer contract expansion: producers still emit only names and phone numbers, while clawdex records which local source saw those values in the person markdown it already owns.

The Go 1.26.4 bump is also intentional: `govulncheck` on Go 1.26.3 reports reachable standard-library findings fixed in Go 1.26.4. This does not need to be split unless maintainers want a separate release-policy change.

## Boundary

This is the clawdex consumer side of the same contact-import slice as `steipete/wacrawl#12` and `openclaw/telecrawl#9`. The crawler owns source-native contact extraction; clawdex owns canonical people and imports by pulling metadata-advertised `contact-export` commands.

This is intentionally a local CLI/control metadata surface, not a graph layer, candidate model, or broad crawler-to-crawler abstraction.

Crawler contact imports do not use display-name-only matches for automatic joins. If a crawler emits a name that matches an existing person but no stronger identifier matches, clawdex creates a separate person for now rather than attaching a phone number to the wrong human.

## Validation

Current head: `1a285a6103c28f1f4fa821f8e1b2e7f59b2a013b`.

Local gates on this head:

- `nix shell nixpkgs#go --command go test -count=1 ./...`
- `nix shell nixpkgs#go --command go test -count=1 -race ./...`
- `git diff --check`
- producer contract checks:
  - `nix shell nixpkgs#go --command go test ./...` in `wacrawl`
  - `nix shell nixpkgs#go --command go test ./...` in `telecrawl`

Copied real-data proof was run locally with full raw outputs kept private because it contains real contact names and phone numbers: `/tmp/clawdex-contact-current-raw-proof.Qxb081/RAW_OUTPUTS_CURRENT_HEAD.md`.

Public aggregate from that raw proof:

- copied Telegram archive source: `2081` contact rows, `693` chats, `54361` messages
- current telecrawl export after conversation-backed filtering: `53` contacts / `53` phone values
- copied WhatsApp archive source: `50` contact rows, `234` chats, `10968` messages
- current wacrawl export: `49` contacts / `49` phone values
- telecrawl import into a clean copied clawdex repo: `53` changes = `51` creates + `2` updates
- wacrawl import after telecrawl: `49` changes = `27` creates + `22` updates
- repeat imports from both sources returned `[]`
- a real stale Telegram peer/contact-table row with no chat/message evidence stayed present in the source DB but did not create a clawdex person
- a real cross-source phone match unified Telegram and WhatsApp names on one clawdex person and recorded both source backrefs
- a real duplicate-phone Telegram case kept both source names on one person rather than creating two people
- local ClawSweeper-style preflight found no accepted code, test, metadata, proof, privacy, or cross-repo contract findings remaining

## Privacy

The payload shape is intentionally only `contacts[].display_name` and `contacts[].phone_numbers`. Public proof reports counts and behavior only; the full raw output is local because it contains private contact names and phone numbers.

for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

target.title: Import contact exports from source crawlers
target.body:
## Summary

- add the clawdex-owned `ContactExport` v0 proto artifact and strict JSON decoder
- add `clawdex import contacts --from <source crawler>`
- discover `contact-export` through crawlkit metadata, require `crawlkit.control.v1`, require JSON/read-only, bind execution to the same `--from` binary, and import only display name plus phone values into the existing `SourceContact` path
- record simple per-source evidence in clawdex person markdown so an existing phone match can still say it was seen in `telecrawl` or `wacrawl`
- keep crawler imports from auto-merging by name alone; crawler imports match by stronger identifiers such as accounts, external IDs, email, or normalized phone
- make crawler import dry-runs match real imports, dedupe phones by normalized phone value, skip cross-person conflicting phone values, and return `[]` for no-op JSON imports
- align the proto package with the v0 contract: `openclaw.clawdex.contactexport.v0`
- move clawdex to Go 1.26.4 so CI `govulncheck` uses the fixed standard library

## Related PRs

- Consumer: https://github.com/openclaw/clawdex/pull/2
- WhatsApp producer: https://github.com/steipete/wacrawl/pull/12
- Telegram producer: https://github.com/openclaw/telecrawl/pull/9

These three PRs are one contact-export v0 slice. Source crawlers own source-native contact extraction; clawdex owns canonical people and imports by pulling the crawler metadata `contact-export` command. They should land in lockstep; if the command name, metadata argv, envelope, or field names change in one repo, all three should change together.

## Intent

The discoverable local `contact-export` metadata command is intentional. Clawdex discovering and running that read-only command is intentional. Local opt-in import of stored contact display names and phone numbers into clawdex is the feature boundary, not an accidental privacy expansion.

The v0 producer contract is deliberately small: `crawlkit.control.v1`, command name `contact-export`, JSON envelope `contacts`, fields `display_name` and `phone_numbers`, and read-only metadata argv. This PR intentionally does not add graph/candidate/source-id/JID/username/ranking fields or a generic crawler-to-crawler protocol.

Clawdex records local-only source evidence after import. That is not a producer contract expansion: producers still emit only names and phone numbers, while clawdex records which local source saw those values in the person markdown it already owns.

The Go 1.26.4 bump is also intentional: `govulncheck` on Go 1.26.3 reports reachable standard-library findings fixed in Go 1.26.4. This does not need to be split unless maintainers want a separate release-policy change.

## Boundary

This is the clawdex consumer side of the same contact-import slice as `steipete/wacrawl#12` and `openclaw/telecrawl#9`. The crawler owns source-native contact extraction; clawdex owns canonical people and imports by pulling metadata-advertised `contact-export` commands.

This is intentionally a local CLI/control metadata surface, not a graph layer, candidate model, or broad crawler-to-crawler abstraction.

Crawler contact imports do not use display-name-only matches for automatic joins. If a crawler emits a name that matches an existing person but no stronger identifier matches, clawdex creates a separate person for now rather than attaching a phone number to the wrong human.

## Validation

Current head: `1a285a6103c28f1f4fa821f8e1b2e7f59b2a013b`.

Local gates on this head:

- `nix shell nixpkgs#go --command go test -count=1 ./...`
- `nix shell nixpkgs#go --command go test -count=1 -race ./...`
- `git diff --check`
- producer contract checks:
  - `nix shell nixpkgs#go --command go test ./...` in `wacrawl`
  - `nix shell nixpkgs#go --command go test ./...` in `telecrawl`

Copied real-data proof was run locally with full raw outputs kept private because it contains real contact names and phone numbers: `/tmp/clawdex-contact-current-raw-proof.Qxb081/RAW_OUTPUTS_CURRENT_HEAD.md`.

Public aggregate from that raw proof:

- copied Telegram archive source: `2081` contact rows, `693` chats, `54361` messages
- current telecrawl export after conversation-backed filtering: `53` contacts / `53` phone values
- copied WhatsApp archive source: `50` contact rows, `234` chats, `10968` messages
- current wacrawl export: `49` contacts / `49` phone values
- telecrawl import into a clean copied clawdex repo: `53` changes = `51` creates + `2` updates
- wacrawl import after telecrawl: `49` changes = `27` creates + `22` updates
- repeat imports from both sources returned `[]`
- a real stale Telegram peer/contact-table row with no chat/message evidence stayed present in the source DB but did not create a clawdex person
- a real cross-source phone match unified Telegram and WhatsApp names on one clawdex person and recorded both source backrefs
- a real duplicate-phone Telegram case kept both source names on one person rather than creating two people
- local ClawSweeper-style preflight found no accepted code, test, metadata, proof, privacy, or cross-repo contract findings remaining

## Privacy

The payload shape is intentionally only `contacts[].display_name` and `contacts[].phone_numbers`. Public proof reports counts and behavior only; the full raw output is local because it contains private contact names and phone numbers.

for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels
